description: File handle management with begin/rescue/ensure vs early return leak

expected.notes: leaky_process returns nil on empty data without closing file handle
File.open protected by ensure block — state-resource-leak must NOT fire (regression guard for Ruby ensure)
puts in rescue handler — cfg-unreachable-sink must NOT fire (rescue is reachable on exception, regression guard)
noise: state engine does not yet model Ruby ensure blocks; possible-leak is conservative — aspirational suppression
rescue IOError => e is modeled as a tainted-exception source; puts e.message at line 7 is an output sink — medium-severity flow incidental to this CFG fixture, aspirationally suppressible
